hypothesis.statement: We test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned on the known-ahead intraday session-position windows around the RTH open and the RTH close (the first/last minutes of the regular session, where auction-adjacent flow concentrates) differs from the mid-session. This is a falsifiable intraday-seasonality conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
hypothesis.economic_rationale: Auction-adjacent flow (opening price discovery, closing-auction imbalance into MOC/LOC orders) concentrates at deterministic, known-ahead points of the regular session clock; whether the minutes adjacent to those windows carry a measurable conditional forward pattern is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve. The closing-auction itself is not directly observed without paid imbalance data; the conditioner here is calendar/clock position alone.
hypothesis._note: Index futures trade continuously and do not have an equity-style single-price open/close auction; this card conditions on the SESSION-CLOCK windows adjacent to the RTH open/close, not on a literal auction print. Any literal auction-imbalance feed is paid data and is OUT OF SCOPE / deferred.

lookahead_risk.description: The open/close proximity windows are derived entirely from the existing point-in-time minutes_from_rth_open / minutes_to_rth_close clocks, which are known-ahead given the published session template. The hazard is using a fixed wall-clock cutoff that is wrong on EARLY_CLOSE sessions, where the close shifts earlier.
lookahead_risk.mitigation: Define the open/close proximity windows as thresholds on the existing minutes_from_rth_open / minutes_to_rth_close features (which already account for the actual session window via the session template and SessionWindowState classification), NOT as fixed wall-clock times, so EARLY_CLOSE sessions are handled correctly. No new feature and no metadata surface is required; the windows are pure thresholds on existing point-in-time clocks.

expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.statement: Conditioning on a known-ahead session-clock position is structurally distinct from the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms conditioned on. The MAIN orthogonality risk here is that vwap_session (a dead mechanism) is itself session-anchored, so an into-the-close effect could be adjacent to vwap_session dynamics; orthogonality must be demonstrated, not assumed. This is the weakest-orthogonality intraday card.
shared_inputs_with_dead: session-position clock is adjacent substrate to the (dead) vwap_session mechanism, which is also session-anchored; this is conditioning adjacency, not a shared engineered input
expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.diagnostic_check: Residualize forward outcomes against the vwap_session and regime dead-mechanism factors before scoring the open/close-proximity conditioner, and report the conditioner's marginal contribution on the residual. Confirm the open and close windows are tested as one pre-registered conditioner (two thresholds), not split into two separate hypotheses, to avoid inflating the surface.

capacity_turnover_note: Open and close proximity windows occur in every RTH session (roughly 250/year), so occurrence count is high relative to the calendar-event cards, but the per-session conditional sample is small (a few minutes each). Turnover would be high if traded near the close (one decision per session); this is a turnover-fragile candidate. Pool across the index-futures family per Track-B. No capacity/turnover dollar claim.

data_dependency.detail: Fully covered by the existing minutes_from_rth_open / minutes_to_rth_close / rth_segment_flag session features; no new FeatureRequest and no external feed required for the clock-position conditioner. A literal auction-imbalance feed (MOC/LOC imbalance, opening imbalance) is paid data and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE / deferred for this card.

value_free_status.claims: hypothesis pre-registration only; no alpha/tradability/profitability claim
